items.title: IDFC First Bankscam: CBI searches in Chandigarh, Ludhiana
items.summary: The CBI conducted searches in Chandigarh and Ludhiana concerning a Rs 657 crore fund misappropriation. These searches targeted beneficiaries and public servants involved in the alleged fraud. Incriminating documents and digital evidence were recovered during the extensive searches. The investigation was transferred from Haryana's vigilance bureau to the CBI. This fraud involved siphoning government funds through forged fixed deposits and debit transactions.

items.summary: India and Myanmar are enhancing cooperation in rare earths sector. Indian teams visited Myanmar in December 2024 and February 2026. This collaboration aims to secure strategic mineral supplies for India. Myanmar's Kachin state is a key source of heavy rare earths. Both nations see significant win-win opportunities from this partnership.

Earnings were hit by a Rs 240 crore semaglutide API-related impact, including inventory provisions, while higher solvent and freight costs due to the Middle East conflict further squeezed EBITDA margins.

Resilient hedging demand and increased equity index trading volumes fueled this positive financial performance. Despite softer trading in some areas, the company's data services business also showed significant revenue growth. Investor concerns about perpetual futures persist, though some analysts see long-term insignificance. The company is also preparing for a leadership transition with a new CEO appointed.

items.summary: "We'll be hitting that area pretty soon, and very heavily," President Donald Trump said about Iran's underground nuclear facility in Pickaxe Mountain.

items.title: US-India trade deal could be signed in 3-4 months, US official says
items.summary: A U.S.-India trade agreement is nearing completion and could be signed soon. Negotiations are virtually finished, with only U.S. trade investigations pending resolution. These investigations cover unfair trade practices and may lead to retaliatory measures. Once completed, progress is expected for India and other nations. The agreement aims to expand market access and deepen economic ties between both countries.

items.title: Beyond roti, kapda, makaan: Inside India's new consumption story
items.summary: Indian consumers now prioritize experiences and lifestyle choices over basic needs. Average monthly spending has increased across both rural and urban areas. Food now forms a smaller portion of household expenditure, reflecting rising incomes. Younger generations balance saving for homes with immediate lifestyle purchases. Easy credit fuels consumption, increasing household debt levels significantly.

items.title: Economic activity hits 32-month high in June, says Icra; retains Q1 growth forecast at 6.4-6.6%
items.summary: Icra attributed the stronger momentum to the temporary ceasefire between the US and Iran, prolonged construction and mining activity due to a rainfall deficit, and favourable base effects

items.summary: June's fall in inflation will be welcomed by the new prime minister but analysts warn the fall is temporary.

items.title: Karnataka attracts $13 billion in FDI as tech, GCC investments surge
items.summary: Karnataka's foreign direct investment nearly doubled to thirteen billion dollars last financial year. Tech investors and global capability centers significantly boosted Karnataka's investment inflows. Maharashtra, the top destination, experienced a dip in foreign direct investment after two years. Computer software and hardware sectors cornered nearly forty percent of total inflows. Food processing industry saw foreign direct investment grow nearly six times.

items.summary: India is unlikely to face any immediate sugar shortage, as domestic stocks are expected to be sufficient to meet demand until the next crushing season begins, says Atul Chaturvedi of Shree Renuka Sugars.

items.title: Glenfiddich-maker eyes wider India portfolio as UK FTA cuts Scotch prices
items.summary: The India-UK FTA is expected to reduce Scotch whisky prices by 7-10%, allowing William Grant & Sons to attract new consumers and expand its offerings.

items.title: RBI likely used dollar deposit surge to cut record FX forward book, economists say
items.summary: Reserve Bank of India likely used deposit inflows to reduce its forward book. This action helped the central bank manage its near-term dollar obligations effectively. Economists suggest this strategy offset short-dollar positions in the forward market. Inflows attracted over twenty billion dollars, with most from foreign currency deposits. June data will provide further evidence of this reduction in forward liabilities.

items.title: Cabinet clears ₹62,500 cr MPMS scheme: 'Focus now on components, design, research'
India's edible oil imports to surge July-October as supplies shrink before festivals
items.summary: India's edible oil imports will increase from July to October. This rise is due to slower domestic soybean and rapeseed crushing operations. Higher imports are expected to meet demand before the festive season begins. Refiners are actively purchasing palm and soy oil for upcoming shipments. This will support global vegetable oil futures and reduce supplier inventories.

items.summary: Although Indian pharma stocks fell in early trade, analysts called it a “kneejerk reaction” driven by the ambiguity of the announcement and its uncertain timeline.

items.summary: US President Donald Trump's proposed tariffs of up to 200% on imported generic medicines could put pressure on India's pharmaceutical exporters, but the country's cost advantage may help many drugs remain competitive in the US market, according to the Global Trade Research Initiative (GTRI).

items.title: Why are central banks buying more gold? Here's what it means for the global economy
items.summary: Central Banks and Gold: Central banks globally are buying gold at a record pace. This trend is driven by geopolitical risks and inflation concerns. Gold offers diversification and a hedge against currency devaluation. Many nations are also bringing their gold reserves home. This shift signals a long-term strategy for financial resilience.

items.title: Bira 91 founder's exit leaves former employees asking: What about our unpaid dues?
items.summary: Ankur Jain has stepped down after a lender settlement, but former employees say the announcement offers no clarity on pending salaries or statutory dues.

items.title: Trump’s 100% generic drug duty threatens US low-cost supply
items.summary: President Donald Trump has given generic drugmakers two years to shift manufacturing to the US or face 100% import tariffs from August 2028, rising to 200% a year later. The move threatens India's pharmaceutical exports, could disrupt low-cost medicine supplies, raise drug prices, and trigger shortages for millions of American patients.

items.title: Delhi HC upholds Centre's move to take Gujarat offshore block from Vedanta
items.summary: The court dismissed Vedanta's challenge to the Centre's refusal to extend its production sharing contract for the CB-OS/2 block and declined interim relief, paving the way for the government to implement its decision.

items.title: Trump's Generic Drug Tariffs Explained: What it means for Indian pharma before the 200% storm
items.summary: Trump's generic drug tariffs plan provides Indian drugmakers a two-year duty-free window. This allows companies time to reassess manufacturing and supply chain strategies. After this period, tariffs will escalate significantly, impacting export economics. Several Indian firms already have US manufacturing operations for flexibility. The next two years will shape how India serves its largest overseas pharmaceutical market.

items.title: India's coking coal import bill down 39% in three years helped by Russian discounts, low-price global cycle
items.summary: India's coking coal import bill decreased from $19.2 billion in fiscal 2023 to $11.76 billion in FY26, as the country shifted imports from Australia to Russia and the US. Domestic output remains insufficient, which means domestic steelmakers are still exposed to global commodity price cycles.

items.title: Indian conglomerates' new pitch to foreign investors: Build with us from day one
items.summary: Instead of waiting to sell stakes after projects are de-risked, India's largest business groups are increasingly bringing in foreign equity partners at the outset to share risk, reduce capital outlay and fund simultaneous expansion across sectors.

items.title: 'I can't afford to turn the oven on': 7.4m households struggling to buy essentials
items.summary: As Andy Burnham promises “breathing space” for households, millions are unable to buy items such as clothing, heating and food.

items.title: Trade all at sea: Freight rattled by delays, ship shortages and port congestion as West Asia conflict flares up again
items.summary: Renewed conflict in West Asia is significantly disrupting India's exports, with exporters facing container shortages and limited vessel availability. Sea freight rates have surged sharply, doubling or even tripling on several routes. Congestion at key Indian ports, including Mundra and JNPT, has further worsened shipping delays. The disruption has increased logistics costs and delayed critical export shipments.

items.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
items.summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.

items.title: Fertiliser vessels stuck at ports, raise supply fears for peak kharif sowing
items.summary: Fertiliser vessels are stranded at Indian ports, hindering crop nutrient supplies. Sowing has accelerated after rainfall revival, increasing demand for timely distribution. Port congestion and supply chain issues are delaying crucial fertiliser dispatches. West Asia crisis and rerouting of vessels contribute to these significant delays. Industry executives express concerns over potential disruptions to the kharif sowing season.

items.title: Man Tied to $100 Million NJ Deli Scam Gets 21 Months in Prison
items.summary: A twice-convicted felon who helped orchestrate a scheme to inflate the value of a small New Jersey delicatessen to more than $100 million was ordered to spend 21 months behind bars.

items.title: Registration of redevelopment agreement alone doesn’t trigger tax, rules ITAT
items.summary: Mumbai ITAT ruled agreement registration does not mean property receipt. Tax cannot be levied on future property rights under anti-abuse provisions. The tribunal allowed a taxpayer's appeal against a ₹1.38 crore tax addition. Registration creates contractual rights, not actual property possession or receipt. This decision clarifies tax treatment for ongoing redevelopment projects in Mumbai.

items.title: House panel seeks periodic review of competition law framework, cites repeated violations of rules
items.summary: A parliamentary panel recommended reviewing competition law for the digital sector. It called for vigorous enforcement of monetary penalty guidelines by the CCI. The panel also stressed a transparent methodology for calculating penalty amounts. Adequate protection for small businesses from monopolies was also suggested. Enhanced cooperation between the CCI and other regulators was emphasized.

items.title: MCA plans unified digital platform for insolvency ecosystem, seeks stakeholders’ inputs
items.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has proposed creating a unified digital platform for India's insolvency ecosystem to speed up the resolution of stressed companies under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC).

items.title: Jamie Dimon says AI spending will pay off—but 'definitely not' on the timeline you expect
items.summary: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon expresses cautious optimism about AI investments, predicting they will pay off in the long run but warns against complacency due to geopolitical and fiscal risks that could disrupt markets.

items.title: WTO urges India to cut trade-restrictive measures reliance, New Delhi flags global non-tariff barriers
items.summary: World Trade Organization (WTO) Secretariat has urged India to improve its business environment and reduce reliance on high tariffs, import-export controls and other trade-restrictive measures to boost competitiveness and attract more foreign investment ahead of India's 8th Trade Policy Review.

items.title: Mahindra Finance Q1 profit jumps 75% to ₹927 crore on higher interest margin, lower provisions
items.summary: Mahindra and Mahindra Financial Services on Tuesday reported a 75% jump in its consolidated net profit at ₹927 crore in the June quarter of FY27 on higher net interest margins and lower provisions.

items.title: TVS, Bajaj report record quarterly revenue, see demand momentum continuing
items.summary: Both companies attributed their strong performance to sustained domestic demand following GST cuts and robust export growth, which helped offset higher fuel prices and logistics disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict.

items.title: Privatise all CPSEs; bring oil, gas, alcohol under GST: US think tank wishlist for India
items.summary: A US think tank proposed thirty reforms to boost India's economic growth. These suggestions include a ten-year plan for privatizing all CPSEs. The government has already completed three of these recommended reforms. Difficult reforms involve land acquisition and bringing more sectors under GST. Further initiatives could accelerate India's global competitiveness.

items.title: India's debt-to-GDP ratio moderates to 58.2% in FY26: RS
items.summary: India's debt-to-GDP ratio moderated to 58.2 percent in FY26, showing improved fiscal health. The government's interest payments have declined significantly since the Covid-19 pandemic. Effective capital expenditure for FY27 exceeds fresh debt receipts, indicating asset creation focus. Central government capital expenditure between FY21 and FY26 reached Rs 44.03 lakh crore. Initiatives like PM GatiShakti are strengthening infrastructure development and project implementation.

items.title: Proxy advisory firm flags Meesho’s GST model, seeks Sebi scrutiny
items.summary: InGovern Research Services says Meesho’s logistics arm derives a material benefit from its GST treatment, while the company rejects the claims and says it remains fully compliant with tax and disclosure laws.

items.title: Rupee value is market-driven, has no target band against US dollar: FM Sitharaman
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated the rupee's value is market-determined. The Reserve Bank of India monitors foreign exchange markets and intervenes when needed. Several measures are in place to boost foreign exchange inflows into India. These initiatives aim to ease pressure on the Indian rupee. The government closely tracks economic parameters and their implications.

items.title: RBI issues draft for future-ready rules related to foreign investments
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has proposed new foreign investment rules. These simplified rules aim to enhance clarity and reduce complexity. Indian companies may soon list shares on foreign stock exchanges. Foreign investors can also make investments on repatriation or non-repatriation bases. The central bank is seeking public comments on these draft regulations.

items.title: India engaged with the US, studying all the developments: Govt tells Parliament
items.summary: The government told Parliament it is closely monitoring developments in its trade relationship with the US and remains engaged with Washington. It noted that while US reciprocal tariffs were struck down by the Supreme Court, a 10% tariff on certain products remains in force.

items.title: From Assam to Nilgiri: How regional teas are becoming premium brands
items.summary: India's tea market is shifting towards premium products and regional diversity. Gen Z and millennials are driving this growth, seeking authenticity and unique flavors. Regional teas from Assam, Darjeeling, and Nilgiri are gaining new recognition. Changing lifestyles and modern retail are expanding tea consumption and accessibility. Innovation and heritage are key for the industry's future growth.

items.title: Indian reservoir water level drops 39% from year ago even as monsoon rainfall improves
items.summary: The uneven distribution of this season's monsoon rain has left India's major reservoirs perilously low, affecting agricultural prospects and drinking water supplies. Experts warn of severe impacts on both kharif and rabi crops if rainfall remains inconsistent.
